read_plan.preview: **Out-of-tier texts**: the most commercially significant category. [[Rare Grimoire|Rare]]-tier and above grimoires are not freely sold in either kingdom — [[Terravelle]] holds them in institutional libraries, and [[Aural

The black market for grimoires is not primarily operated by criminals in the sense of people who specialize in crime. It is operated primarily by practitioners and traders who have access to originals and treat the infor

Both kingdoms are aware the informal market exists. Neither has invested heavily in suppressing it. The calculation is roughly the same on both sides: the Common tier is not worth suppressing because it doesn't need to b
Current enforcement and market behavior are best understood as a linked risk chain rather than isolated fraud events.

The numeric tier is the spell's **Flux handling requirement**. Each tier is roughly an order-of-magnitude jump in difficulty, capacity, stability, and precision. Every few tiers feels like a categorically different class
_Barely any Flux. The threshold most people never bother to formally test._
_A small, usable trickle. The first tier where deliberate spellwork is practical._
_The world average. The first tier where magic becomes genuinely useful in daily life._

Auralis society leans toward a soft caste structure, where social standing is loosely tied to magical ability. This is not a rigid hierarchy of law — high-tier casters do not hold formal political office by virtue of the

Auralis embraces the full spectrum of [[Discipline|Disciplines]], including those that make other cultures uncomfortable. Experimentation with Mind and Soul disciplines is not openly banned and in certain scholarly or mi

The principal settlements of Auralis are [[Solhaven]] (the capital, built up coastal terraces on the western shore) and [[Emberfall]] (the interior research and agricultural center). Auralis has several additional port c
Solhaven approaches a million residents and is the most vertically stratified city in Solumora. Its terrace zones function as distinct social worlds stacked on each other.

Terravelle society values stability and efficiency above all. Where other cultures may chase magical prestige, Terravelle embraces the philosophy that the best spell is the one that accomplishes the task with the least e

Low to mid-tier casting is common and unremarkable. [[Grimoires]] here tend to favor Triangle and Square sigil shapes — fast, safe, reproducible, and affordable in [[Flux]]. [[Discipline|Disciplines]] with predictable fa

[[Terravelle]] and [[Auralis]] share a complicated relationship shaped by fundamentally opposed philosophies. Where Auralis pursues magical innovation and supremacy, Terravelle prizes restraint and function. The [[Equato

The principal settlements of Terravelle are [[Valdenmoor]] (the capital, in the central river valley), [[Ashford]] (the primary eastern port), and [[Greyveil]] (the northern waypoint at the edge of the settled belt). Doz

Zakros sits directly on Solumora's equator, a belt of hostile terrain several weeks wide for any traveler moving north to south. It is not a single uniform landscape â€” dune fields, cracked salt flats, exposed rock shel

There are better routes and worse ones. No route is safe. The better paths â€” known to experienced guides, jealously kept, and sold for considerable [[Solumora|Watts]] â€” reduce crossing time and offer slightly more pr

The desert's ambient [[Flux]] distortion is the hard constraint on who can survive a crossing without specialist support. [[Control Tier|T0]] practitioners have no Flux capacity and cannot cast any of the protection effe

Zakros is not lifeless. Its ecosystem is built almost entirely on insects and arachnids, most of which are [[Flux]]-fueled â€” drawing ambient energy from the desert rather than relying solely on biological food chains.

Each [[Sigils|sigil]] in a grimoire is drawn with full precision. A caster who meets the [[Control Tier]] requirement for a given spell can activate it directly from the page, or copy it onto another surface for single u
Grimoires are categorized by the rarity of the [[Spells|spells]] they contain, reflecting the [[Control Tier]] required to use them:

In [[Terravelle]], grimoires are treated as practical tools — functional, affordable at the Common tier, and widely distributed. High-tier grimoires exist but are held in institutional libraries rather than private hands

Grimoires are the mechanism by which [[Flux]] capability is distributed broadly rather than confined to high-tier practitioners. An entire economy exists around their production, authentication, distribution, and trade.

Uncommon spells require [[Control Tier]] T3–T4 and represent the threshold where casual Flux use ends and deliberate craft begins. A skilled Formulist can manage these, but they demand real training and precision. Most c

Expansion Faction members are not unified by any single belief about the ancient civilization or the nature of [[Flux]]. They are unified by the assessment that [[Auralis]]'s current position relative to [[Terravelle]] r

The Expansion Faction and the [[The Culmination Faction|Culmination Faction]] vote together on most major Council decisions and against each other on almost none. This is not ideological alignment — they want different t

The Ashford Blockade, approximately forty-five years ago, was the Expansion Faction's most ambitious early action — a systematic program of trade restrictions designed to extract strategic concessions from [[Terravelle]]

Rare spells require [[Control Tier]] T5–T6 and are the exclusive territory of Channelers. At this level the exponential nature of [[Flux]] scaling means these casters are categorically different from the population below

Current [[Flux]] research is dominated by institutional networks in both kingdoms, each organized around distinct philosophical priorities and strategic orientations. **Contemporary military preparation is increasingly r

**[[Crestward Flux Institute]]** pursues cost reduction, parallel execution patterns, and methods to achieve higher-tier effects through coordinated low-tier practitioner teams. Leadership: [[Casten Miel]] ([[Control Tie

**[[Northern Terravelle Academy]]** emphasizes practical settlement-scale application and coordinated research on [[Rift Incursions|rift mechanics]]. The Academy operates independently of state control (through funding d

**Independent scholars** operate throughout both kingdoms, conducting collaborative research that neither kingdom prioritizes internally. [[Kedn Rast]] ([[Control Tier|T3]]) exemplifies this institutional model, conducti

Terravelle's regulation flows through the [[Trade Guilds]]. The core mechanism is certification: grimoires sold through commercial channels carry a guild mark that indicates they have been reviewed against a registered m

Auralis regulates grimoires through access control rather than certification. The [[The Council of Auralis|Council]] does not operate a public certification system. Instead, it controls distribution of [[Rare Grimoire|Ra

Neither system has effective jurisdiction over copies that cross [[Equatorial Desert|Desert Zakros]]. A spell developed in Terravelle and certified through the guild system has no standing in Auralis's Scholar network an

Both kingdoms maintain informal restrictions on certain spell disciplines — primarily high-tier mind-affecting spells, specific Flux-structural manipulation tools, and spells that have been used in documented atrocities

The Compact's model is cache maintenance. At seven ruins sites across the desert interior, the Compact keeps sealed weather-resistant depots: preserved food, sealed water containers, pre-charged thermal grimoire suppleme

The Relay-Stage Crossing method is the Compact's primary client draw. It is suitable for parties who cannot sustain the full Flux load of an unassisted crossing but need to move cargo or information that cannot wait for

Neither [[Terravelle]] nor [[Auralis]] officially acknowledges the Compact's existence. Both kingdoms have used its services. The [[The Advancement Corps|Advancement Corps]] has used it twice that Compact operators are a

The Compact's single significant failure mode is cache compromise. A destroyed or looted cache along a planned relay route turns a survivable crossing into an emergency. The Compact's track record is good but not perfect
